title: Above Crow占퐏 Nest - Sydney
content:
혻혻혻혻A blanket low and leaden,
혻혻혻혻Though rent across the west,
혻혻혻혻Whose darkness seems to deaden
혻혻혻혻The brightest and the best;
혻혻혻혻A sunset white and staring
혻혻혻혻On cloud-wrecks far away,
혻혻혻혻And haggard house-walls glaring
혻혻혻혻A farewell to the day.

혻혻혻혻A light on tower and steeple,
혻혻혻혻Where sun no longer shines,
혻혻혻혻My people, Oh my people!
혻혻혻혻Rise up and read the signs!
혻혻혻혻Low looms the nearer high-line
혻혻혻혻(No sign of star or moon),
혻혻혻혻The horseman on the skyline
혻혻혻혻Rode hard this afternoon!

혻혻혻혻(Is he, and who shall know it?,
혻혻혻혻The spectre of a scout?
혻혻혻혻The spirit of a poet,
혻혻혻혻Whose truths were met with doubt?
혻혻혻혻Who sought and who succeeded
혻혻혻혻In marking danger占퐏 track,
혻혻혻혻Whose warnings were unheeded
혻혻혻혻Till all the sky was black?)

혻혻혻혻It is a shameful story
혻혻혻혻For our young, generous home,
혻혻혻혻Without the rise and glory
혻혻혻혻We占폻 go as Greece and Rome.
혻혻혻혻Without the sacrifices
혻혻혻혻That make a nation占퐏 name,
혻혻혻혻The elder nation占퐏 vices
혻혻혻혻And luxuries we claim.

혻혻혻혻Grown vain without a conquest,
혻혻혻혻And sure without a fort,
혻혻혻혻And maddened in the one quest
혻혻혻혻For pleasure or for sport.
혻혻혻혻Self-blinded to our starkness
혻혻혻혻We占폻 fling the time away
혻혻혻혻To fight, half-armed, in darkness
혻혻혻혻Who should be armed to-day.

혻혻혻혻This song is for the city,
혻혻혻혻The city in its pride,
혻혻혻혻The coming time shall pity
혻혻혻혻And shield the countryside.
혻혻혻혻Shall we live in the present
혻혻혻혻Till fearful war-clouds loom,
혻혻혻혻And till the sullen peasant
혻혻혻혻Shall leave us to our doom?

혻혻혻혻Cloud-fortresses titanic
혻혻혻혻Along the western sky,
혻혻혻혻The tired, bowed mechanic
혻혻혻혻And pallid clerk flit by.
혻혻혻혻Lit by a light unhealthy,
혻혻혻혻The ghastly after-glare,
혻혻혻혻The veiled and goggled wealthy
혻혻혻혻Drive fast, they know not where.

혻혻혻혻Night占퐏 sullen spirit rouses,
혻혻혻혻The darkening gables lour
혻혻혻혻From ugly four-roomed houses
혻혻혻혻Verandah占폻 windows glower;
혻혻혻혻The last long day-stare dies on
혻혻혻혻The scrub-ridged western side,
혻혻혻혻And round the near horizon
혻혻혻혻The spectral horsemen ride.
meaning: About: Henry Lawson (17 June 1867 - 2 September 1922) was an Australian writer and poet. Along with his contemporary Banjo Paterson, Lawson is among the best-known Australian poets and fiction writers of the colonial period.
